title: Starmer Watch — Pressure Index
description: Composite pressure index, top news, and market signals for Keir Starmer's leadership.

items.title: Pressure Index 62/100 (elevated)
items.content_text: Public pressure bloc is above the modeled 81 MP trigger. Pressure Index 62/100 (elevated). Polymarket's top Starmer-exit signal is 29.5%.

items.title:
Bluesky · @stephenkb.bsky.social: I would puit Starmer above Johnson on the grounds that the ranking order for actions as Prime Minister should go ...
1) Doing good things.
2) Trying to do good things but failing.
3) Doing nothing.
4) Doing bad things.
items.content_text:
I would puit Starmer above Johnson on the grounds that the ranking order for actions as Prime Minister should go ...
1) Doing good things.
2) Trying to do good things but failing.
3) Doing nothing.
4) Doing bad things.

items.title: Bluesky · @iandunt.bsky.social: This is a really good, sensible policy. I would give my right leg for the Labour leadership fight to be composed of ideas like this.

items.title:
Bluesky · @stephenkb.bsky.social: So much UK political opinion is literally just whether someone "looks right" for the role. I suppose it is a mark of progress that Sunak met this criterion as much as Starmer does.

As the below implies, this 'right s...

So much UK political opinion is literally just whether someone "looks right" for the role. I suppose it is a mark of progress that Sunak met this criterion as much as Starmer does.

items.title: Bluesky · @stephenkb.bsky.social: When did Starmer or Sunak try to 'restore sanity'? Sunak insisted on two highly irresponsible budgets, dismembered HS2 in a costly and inefficient way, and wanted to exit from net zero, and Starmer doesn't *do* anything.
items.content_text: When did Starmer or Sunak try to 'restore sanity'? Sunak insisted on two highly irresponsible budgets, dismembered HS2 in a costly and inefficient way, and wanted to exit from net zero, and Starmer doesn't *do* anything.
